evals.expected_output: Reports one standard_rule1 violation for async raw this capture without visible WeakPtr, cancellation, or owner lifetime guarantee.
expectations: Output is valid JSON and follows the SKILL.md schema without Markdown.
At least one violation has rule_id standard_rule1 and rule_level P1.
The evidence mentions the async PostTask boundary and base::Unretained(this).
The issue or impact explains that Stop/delete this can make the posted callback dereference a destroyed object.
The required_fix recommends WeakPtr, cancellation, or equivalent lifetime protection.

evals.expected_output: Reports one standard_rule2 violation for directly touching GPU resource state from an IPC/mojo callback sequence.
expectations: At least one violation has rule_id standard_rule2 and rule_level P1.
The evidence identifies the IPC sequence in BindReceiverOnIpcThread or OnCreateSharedImage.
The evidence identifies shared_image_manager_->CreateSharedImage as GPU resource work.
The required_fix says to post GPU work to the GPU task runner or GPU sequence.
The output does not replace this with a generic missing-thread-check finding.

evals.expected_output: Reports standard_rule3 because compositor_gpu_thread_ is directly invoked without DrDC sequence evidence or dispatch.
expectations: At least one violation has rule_id standard_rule3 and rule_level P1.
The evidence names compositor_gpu_thread_->ScheduleComposite.
The issue explains that compositor_gpu_thread_ access must be confined to the DrDC/GPU sequence.
The required_fix recommends dispatching to the DrDC task runner or verified owner sequence.

evals.expected_output: Reports standard_rule4 when a worker-thread entry directly invokes an external NDK listener without UI-thread dispatch.
expectations: At least one violation has rule_id standard_rule4 and rule_level P2.
The evidence identifies OnNativeColorResultFromWorker or worker_task_runner_ as non-UI sequence evidence.
The evidence identifies OH_NativeColorPicker_ColorSelected or OH_NativeColorPicker_ColorSelectionCanceled as external NDK listener invocation.
The issue explains that the external listener call lacks UI-thread dispatch or UI-thread proof.
The required_fix recommends checking UI thread or posting to UI before invoking the NDK listener.

evals.expected_output: Reports standard_rule5 because worker-thread code directly touches BrowserContext/NWeb-adjacent preference state.
expectations: At least one violation has rule_id standard_rule5 and rule_level P2.
The evidence identifies StartFromWorker as worker sequence evidence.
The evidence identifies browser_context_->GetPrefs() access as the constrained NWeb/BrowserContext-related object.
The required_fix recommends posting to UI before BrowserContext preference state access.

evals.expected_output: Reports standard_rule6 for direct Chromium UI-thread object access from a worker callback.
expectations: At least one violation has rule_id standard_rule6 and rule_level P1.
The evidence identifies worker_task_runner_ or OnStartLayoutWriteFromWorker as non-UI sequence evidence.
The evidence identifies RenderFrameHost or WebContents access.
The issue explains that WebContents/RenderFrameHost are UI-thread objects.
The required_fix recommends posting to UI and resolving the live object there, preferably by id.

evals.expected_output: Reports standard_rule7 because mojo::Connector is created on IO sequence and used on worker sequence.
expectations: At least one violation has rule_id standard_rule7 and rule_level P1.
The evidence identifies BindOnIoSequence as creation or owner sequence evidence.
The evidence identifies SendFromWorker and connector_->Accept as different-sequence use.
The required_fix recommends returning connector use to its owner sequence.

evals.expected_output: Reports standard_rule8 because Audio stream lifecycle work is invoked from UI instead of Audio thread.
expectations: At least one violation has rule_id standard_rule8 and rule_level P2.
The evidence identifies OnUiResume as UI-thread entry evidence.
The evidence identifies Start as an Audio lifecycle operation.
The required_fix recommends posting to audio_task_runner_ or otherwise running on the Audio thread.

evals.expected_output: Reports standard_rule9 because CEF_POST_TASK schedules Web-instance-dependent work before proving the browser/Web instance exists.
expectations: At least one violation has rule_id standard_rule9 and rule_level P2.
The evidence identifies CEF_POST_TASK and the posted SetRotationType task.
The evidence identifies browser_ or browser_->GetHost() as the Web instance dependency.
The issue explains the missing browser/Web instance creation or availability check before posting.
The required_fix recommends checking the Web/browser instance before posting the task.

evals.expected_output: Reports standard_rule10 because scoped_refptr crosses threads while the managed object uses base::RefCounted rather than thread-safe ref counting.
expectations: At least one violation has rule_id standard_rule10 and rule_level P1.
The evidence identifies factory_ crossing from UI sequence into worker_task_runner_->PostTask.
The evidence identifies BackendFactory as base::RefCounted, not base::RefCountedThreadSafe.
The issue does not claim scoped_refptr itself is always unsafe.
The required_fix recommends thread-safe ref counting or confining ownership and release to one sequence.

evals.expected_output: Reports no violation because WebContents is accessed on UI and only an integer id crosses to IO.
expectations: Output is valid JSON and follows the SKILL.md schema without Markdown.
summary.total_violations is 0.
violations is an empty array.
The output does not report standard_rule6 for stable id transfer.

evals.expected_output: Reports no violation because the current file lacks entry-thread, Audio task runner, or object-thread evidence.
expectations: summary.total_violations is 0.
violations is an empty array.
The output does not report standard_rule8 from method names alone.
The output does not add non-schema uncertain, finding, note, or risk fields.
